title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
problem_statement: Serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize it back to the identical binary tree structure.

examples.explanation: The serialized string can be used to reconstruct the same tree.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4]
intuition: Use preorder traversal with null markers for serialization, then deserialize by consuming the tokens in the same order to rebuild the tree.

approaches.name: Preorder serialization with null markers
approaches.description: Encode the tree using DFS and placeholders for null children, then reconstruct the tree by consuming tokens sequentially.
approaches.why_not: Level order serialization also works, but preorder is easier to match with recursive deserialization.
interview_commentary: Preorder traversal records nodes before their children.
Null markers are essential to preserve missing children.
Deserialization reads tokens in the same preorder structure.

interview_qa.a: They preserve the exact tree shape and distinguish missing children.
Preorder records the current node before its children, which matches recursive reconstruction order.

interview_qa.q: How does deserialization use the token order?
interview_qa.a: It reads tokens sequentially and rebuilds the tree structure recursively.

interview_qa.q: Can this be done with level order?
interview_qa.a: Yes, but then you need a queue during serialization and careful handling of null placeholders.
